steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must refute the claim that slow TV is boring, and must do so using a quotation. The answer must both cite Heller's praise and position it as a direct counter to the 'boring' label.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'While some have praised slow TV for affording "a visceral kind of armchair tourism," others have called it "the world's most boring television."': presents both sides without refuting — it concedes the boring claim rather than countering it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Far from boring, slow TV programs can provide "a visceral kind of armchair tourism," as Heller puts it, whereby viewers can watch reindeer migrations in real time.': opens with 'Far from boring,' explicitly refutes the claim, then uses Heller's quote as direct evidence of the opposite. ✓
  • Choice C — 'With broadcasts of ordinary events, like reindeer migrations, occurring in real time, slow TV just might be "the world's most boring television."': reinforces the boring claim instead of refuting it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Reinflytting can afford "a visceral kind of armchair tourism," as Heller puts it.': uses the quote but limits it to one program without framing it as a refutation of the boring claim. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
